overview.summary: Strings are arrays of characters under the hood, but often have special immutable properties.
learningObjectives: Master common string searching / manipulation patterns
overview.interviewRelevance: Many problems test your knowledge of strings and their standard library functions.

paragraphs: Every autocomplete suggestion, every password validation, every plagiarism detector works on strings.
Google's search index stores trillions of character sequences.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: The reason 'Two Sum' has a trivial solution but 'Longest Palindrome' does not is entirely about how string memory works.

definition.technicalDefinition: A string is a sequential collection of characters used to represent text. In languages like Python and Java, they are immutable.

vocabulary.definition: Once created, the contents cannot be changed. Any modification creates a new string.
A contiguous sequence of characters within a string.
A string formed by rearranging the letters of another string.

keyProperties: Accessing a character by index is O(1).
Concatenating two strings is O(N + M) time.
Because they are immutable (in many languages), repetitive concatenation in a loop is computationally expensive O(N^2).

operations.name: Access Character
operations.description: Get the character at a specific index.
operations.visualNote: Jumps directly to the physical address in memory.

operations.name: Concatenation
operations.description: Appends a string to another. In python/java this copies both strings.
operations.visualNote: A completely new larger string is allocated in memory.

operations.name: Substring
operations.description: Extract a continuous slice of the string.
operations.visualNote: A region is copied out to form the new string.

practice.title: Valid Palindrome
practice.keyInsight: Two pointers from the outside in.

practice.title: Find All Anagrams in a String
